pr.title: ft: match ftCo_CaptureWaitHi_Anim, fn_800D79B4, and IASA functions
pr.author: Subarasheese

summary: Matched several previously stubbed ftCommon functions in ftCo_Attack100.c, centered on Super Scope/item rapid-fire behavior and capture-wait-high animation handling. The PR added overlay-style struct access for hard offsets in Fighter data, external float/double constants needed for exact codegen, and refined the it_80291DAC prototype in itsscope.h from unknown parameters to typed Item_GObj*/int arguments.

key_files.reason: Implemented fn_800D79B4, ftCo_ItemScopeRapid_IASA, ftCo_ItemScopeAirRapid_IASA, and ftCo_CaptureWaitHi_Anim; added includes, external constants, and FighterOverlay offset helpers.
Changed it_80291DAC from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to int it_80291DAC(Item_GObj*, int), supporting typed calls from the matched fighter item-scope logic.

what_changed: Added includes for ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureCut.h and it/items/itsscope.h so newly matched capture and item-scope code could call the required functions with visible declarations.
Declared external constants ftCo_804D90D0, ftCo_804D90D4, and ftCo_804D90D8, identified in comments as 0.0f, a decrement value, and a double increment value used by capture animation logic.
Introduced a file-scope FighterOverlay struct mapping specific Fighter offsets such as 0x1A4C, 0x1A58, 0x2340, 0x2344, 0x2348, and 0x234C for direct offset-based access.

Implemented fn_800D79B4 with item_gobj checks, A-button input handling, timer accumulation, flag updates, calls to it_80291DAC and it_80291CF4, and callback dispatch through cb_ground/cb_air depending on timer and item state.
Implemented ftCo_ItemScopeRapid_IASA and ftCo_ItemScopeAirRapid_IASA to set the fighter field at offset 0x2340 from p_ftCommonData+0x5BC when HSD_PAD_A is held.

Implemented ftCo_CaptureWaitHi_Anim to update capture timers, decrement capture wait data using p_ftCommonData offsets, call ftCommon_GrabMash, trigger capture-cut/escape transitions, and adjust animation rate with ftAnim_SetAnimRate.
Updated itsscope.h so it_80291DAC has the concrete signature int it_80291DAC(Item_GObj*, int) instead of an unknown placeholder.
smart_moves: Used overlay structs to force direct loads/stores at exact Fighter offsets instead of relying on incomplete or differently shaped Fighter struct fields, which helped preserve target instruction selection and register behavior.
Used a local FighterOverlay inside fn_800D79B4 specifically for offsets 0x2340 and 0x2344, matching the PR note that this prevented address caching in registers such as r26/r27.
Used raw pointer arithmetic for fields like input at 0x65C and p_ftCommonData offsets 0x5B8/0x5BC/0x3A4/0x3A8/0x3B0/0x3B4 where named struct coverage was not sufficient for a match.
Preserved unusual codegen requirements with casts such as reading ftCo_804D9024 as an f32 pointer and using a volatile f32 comparison on x2344 in ftCo_CaptureWaitHi_Anim.
Typed it_80291DAC only as far as the evidence supported from its new call site: Item_GObj* plus int input returning int.
decomp_lessons: For fighter fields not yet represented safely in headers, a narrow overlay struct can be a practical matching tactic, especially when exact offset access affects load/store scheduling or register allocation.

When matching code that uses global constants, the declared type matters: this PR distinguishes f32 constants from an f64 increment to reproduce double-precision arithmetic in ftCo_CaptureWaitHi_Anim.

IASA functions may require subtle access-pattern differences even when logic is nearly identical; the ground rapid IASA uses GET_FIGHTER while the air rapid IASA reads the Fighter pointer from gobj+0x2C to match assembly.
Common-data offsets can be left as explicit pointer arithmetic when the relevant struct names or field layout are not yet available, but these offsets should remain searchable for future cleanup.
Header prototype improvements are useful matching enablers: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with a concrete signature can document item/fighter API boundaries and reduce casts at call sites.
naming_conventions: Matched functions retain project-style names such as ftCo_CaptureWaitHi_Anim, ftCo_ItemScopeRapid_IASA, and ftCo_ItemScopeAirRapid_IASA.
Unknown or not-yet-semantic helper names remain address-based, including fn_800D79B4, fn_800DC044, and fn_800DC070.
Temporary overlay fields use offset-derived names like x1A4C, x1A58, x2340, x2344, x2348, and x234C rather than speculative semantic names.
Item Super Scope APIs keep the it_ prefix and address-style names, with it_80291DAC gaining typed parameters while retaining its address-derived symbol.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Declared extern f32/f64 constants to drive exact floating-point and double-precision operations.
Used FighterOverlay casts to produce direct offset access for Fighter fields at 0x1A4C, 0x1A58, and 0x2340-0x234C.
Used a local overlay in fn_800D79B4 to target timer at 0x2340 and flag at 0x2344.
Used raw byte-pointer arithmetic for input and p_ftCommonData offsets where struct typing would likely alter codegen.
Used a cast to read an external int symbol ftCo_804D9024 as f32 to match an lfs-style load noted in the PR context.
Used volatile f32 access for fp_ovl->x2344 in ftCo_CaptureWaitHi_Anim to preserve comparison/load behavior.
Inserted a dummy local byte array in ftCo_CaptureWaitHi_Anim, likely to influence stack layout; evidence is present in the diff but the exact reason is not explicitly documented.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR slice.
The human text notes that overlay structs and casts were intentional matching devices, but there is no separate reviewer feedback to attribute.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace FighterOverlay x2340/x2344/x2348/x234C with named Fighter fields.
Search for later cleanup of p_ftCommonData offsets 0x3A4, 0x3A8, 0x3B0, 0x3B4, 0x5B8, and 0x5BC.
Check whether it_80291DAC received a semantic name or more complete documentation in later item Super Scope work.
Look for follow-up matches of ftCo_ItemScopeAirRapid_Anim and neighboring ftCo_Attack100.c stubs.
Search for other PRs using volatile f32 or overlay structs to force matching load behavior.
